# validate.check_collinearity { #fmrimod.validate.check_collinearity }

```python
validate.check_collinearity(X, threshold=0.9)
```

Check a design matrix for multicollinearity.

Computes the pairwise Pearson correlation between all non-intercept,
non-zero-variance columns and flags pairs whose absolute correlation
exceeds the threshold.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name      | Type                                           | Description                                                                                                              | Default    |
|-----------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| X         | EventModel, numpy.ndarray, or pandas.DataFrame | Design matrix to check. Intercept-like columns (named ``'Intercept'``, ``'constant'``, etc.) are automatically excluded. | _required_ |
| threshold | [float](`float`)                               | Absolute correlation above which a pair is flagged as collinear.                                                         | `0.9`      |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type           | Description                                                                                                                                                                    |
|--------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [dict](`dict`) | ``{'ok': bool, 'pairs': DataFrame}`` where ``ok`` is True when no pair exceeds the threshold. The ``pairs`` DataFrame has columns ``regressor_1``, ``regressor_2``, and ``r``. |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> import numpy as np
>>> X = np.column_stack([np.arange(100), np.arange(100) + 0.1])
>>> result = check_collinearity(X, threshold=0.99)
>>> result['ok']
False
```

## See Also {.doc-section .doc-section-see-also}

validate_contrasts : Validate contrast estimability.
